question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MempoolTransaction() does not validate the transaction_identifier.hash format (hex, length, etc.) before returning ErrNotImplemented to send requests with hash values containing special characters that are logged by the server, causing log injection attacks that corrupt server logs and hide evidence of ongoing transaction confirmation attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the Mempool() function signature accepts *types.NetworkRequest but the parameter is discarded to send requests with a network_identifier.sub_network_identifier containing an extremely long string, causing the asserter to allocate a large string buffer during validation and exhaust memory, crashing the server and halting transaction confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the deterministic behavior of Mempool() (always returns the same error) to perform a timing side-channel attack, measuring response times to infer server load and identify optimal windows to launch a DoS attack that maximally disrupts transaction confirmation processing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: MempoolTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MempoolTransaction() returns ErrNotImplemented without any transaction-specific context to cause Rosetta clients that use the mempool to implement transaction deduplication to fail to detect duplicate submissions, resulting in double-spend transactions being submitted to the network and causing consensus failures that halt transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/mempool_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: Mempool()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the /mempool endpoint is accessible without any authentication in the online router to perform reconnaissance on the server's error response format, then craft subsequent attacks against /construction/submit that exploit the same error handling patterns to cause transaction submission failures and network-wide confirmation halts?
